title: Very low test coverage (5.9%) despite passing build
evidence: scores.json: test_coverage=0.059, defect_rate=1.0. mcp_test.go:14-21 loadTestDB t.Fatalf's without data/kaggle, and that dir is absent from the archive — so the 25 data-dependent tests exercise almost no code; only the 4 pure unit tests (normalize/parse) contribute.
suggestion: Add small fixture CSVs committed alongside tests (or embed via testdata/) so query/loader code is actually exercised independent of the external dataset.

title: TestSearchMatches_TeamAndSeason can skip silently
evidence: mcp_test.go:210 t.Skip("No Palmeiras matches in 2023 - dataset may not include this year")
suggestion: Assert against a season/team known to exist in the fixture so the test always verifies behavior instead of skipping.

title: TestGetTeamStats_HomeRecord can skip silently
suggestion: Use a team/season guaranteed present in the data so the home-record path is always tested.

title: TestParseDate asserts nothing
evidence: mcp_test.go:66-85 — on success it does `_ = t` to suppress the unused warning and never compares t.Year() to wantYear; on parse error it `continue`s. The test passes regardless of correctness.
suggestion: Fail the test when parseDate errors on a supported format and assert t.Year() == c.wantYear.

title: Team-name matching does not fold accents
evidence: normalize.go:27 teamMatches lowercases but never strips diacritics; mcp_test.go:56 documents that `teamMatches("América - MG", "America")` returns false. TASK.md (lines 317-322) requires handling accented Portuguese names.
suggestion: Normalize accents (e.g. unicode NFD + strip combining marks) before comparison so 'America' matches 'América'.

title: Match filtering supports season only, not arbitrary date ranges
evidence: tools.go:236 SearchMatches filters by season equality; there is no from/to date-range parameter. R4 asks for 'date range and/or season' (season satisfies it, range is unimplemented).
suggestion: Add optional start_date/end_date params to search_matches for the date-range half of the requirement.

title: Robust dual JSON-RPC framing and zero external dependencies
evidence: server.go:94-143 readMessage auto-detects Content-Length vs newline-delimited JSON; go.mod declares no third-party modules (stdlib only).
suggestion: None — noting as a positive beyond the spec.
